conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about Alex's situation.

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-levels would typically count as non-advanced education.

Does Alex live with you, or does he live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your own circumstances.

Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're looking after him and either:
- Living with him in the UK, or
- Contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after him (if he lives elsewhere for part of the time)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. He lives with me here in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital or in the care of a local authority?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He's at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Are you or your partner subject to immigration control, or do you have the right to reside in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I have the right to reside in the UK, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me review the eligibility criteria to make a final assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility rules, I can now provide you with a decision on your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels, which counts as approved non-advanced education
- He lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for him
- He is not in hospital or care
- You have the right to reside in the UK

Since Alex is under 20 and remains in approved education, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government service. If you need any further information or have questions, feel free to contact the Child Benefit Office.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels, which qualifies as approved non-advanced education. According to the eligibility rules, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with you in the UK (Manchester), you are responsible for him, he is not in hospital or care, and you have the right to reside in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (18 years old), who is studying A-levels (approved non-advanced education). Alex lives with the claimant in Manchester, UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex, has the right to reside in the UK, and Alex is not in hospital or care. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
